LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. INSUFFICIENT FACTS ALLEGED THAT PLAINTIFF HAS EXHAUSTED HIS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. NATIONAL ORIGIN AND RELIGION ALLEGATIONS MUST BE STATED IN SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN AND AMBIGUOUS. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT. INSUFFICIENT FACTS ALLEGED THAT PLAINTIFF HAS EXHAUSTED HIS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. NATIONAL ORIGIN AND RELIGION ALLEGATIONS MUST BE STATED IN SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN AND AMBIGUOUS. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE. MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Richard M. Rogers, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: NAGARAJ NAIDU
Jonathan D. Martin, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: California Public Utilities Commission

Hearing held on PLAINTIFF NAGARAJ NAIDU's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents:
a. As to Interrogatory no. 19, granted, without prejudice to any objection, or motion in limine that defendant may raise at the time of trial, tentative adopted.
b. As to Interrogatory no. 20, granted. Defendant shall provide supplemental response identifying those authorized to access the subject document.
c. As to request for production no. 11, off calendar, moot, based on plaintiff's representation that the parties have resolved this issue.
Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Richard M. Rogers, Law Offices of Richard M. Rogers, (415) 981-9788; appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; P. Bobby Shukla, Esq. of Ruiz & Sperow, LLP, (510) 594-7980; appearing for Defendant California Utilities Commission and moving party. Argued and The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. INSUFFICIENT FACTS ALLEGED THAT PLAINTIFF HAS EXHAUSTED HIS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. NATIONAL ORIGIN AND RELIGION ALLEGATIONS MUST BE STATED IN SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN AND AMBIGUOUS. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. Defense counsel to prepare proposed Order for the Court's signature. (302/CWW/VC) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
